[[File:Eye of ayak spell.gif|thumb|250px|A player using the Eye of Ayak's built in spell.]]
The '''eye of Ayak''' is a one-handed [[powered staff]] that requires level 83 [[Magic]] to wield. It is a drop from the [[Doom of Mokhaiotl]] upon completion of delve level 3 and higher.

It is the second strongest powered staff, placing it between the [[Sanguinesti staff]] and [[Tumeken's shadow]] in power. The maximum damage per hit is comparatively low, but it has the fastest attack speed of all powered staves, firing at a rate of 3 ticks (1.8 seconds) compared to the other staves' 4 or 5-tick speed.

The eye of Ayak can be dismantled for 10,000 [[Demon tear]]s, resulting in a loss of {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Demon tear|10000}}-{{GEP|Eye of ayak (uncharged)}}|c}}.

The eye of Ayak cannot be used in [[Castle Wars]], [[Clan Wars]], [[TzHaar Fight Pit]], and [[Soul Wars]], despite all other powered staves being usable against players in those areas.

The eye of Ayak does not lose charges on death in the [[Wilderness]] if the item is [[Items Kept on Death|protected]], even if the death occurs past level 20 Wilderness.

==Special attack==
[[File:Soul Rend.gif|thumb|250px|A player using the eye of Ayak's special attack, ''Soul Rend''.]]
The eye of Ayak has a [[special attack]], ''Soul Rend'', which consumes 50% of the player's special attack energy. The special attack is fired slower than a standard attack, with it being a 5-tick attack rather than 3. As compensation, the player's accuracy is doubled and max hit is increased by 30%.

When it successfully damages a target, the target will lose Magic Defence equal to the amount dealt. This is different compared to Magic levels, but can still provide a noticeable impact when facing an enemy with a low Magic level but high Magic Defence, such as [[General Graardor]]. As Magic Defence is a stat and not a level, NPCs cannot restore drained defence.

Magic Defence cannot be drained below 0, but it will carry over if any stat changes are detected. For example, a player hits 50 with the special on [[Yama]] while he is in his melee stance (-30 Magic Defence). If Yama changes to his magic stance (+60 Magic Defence in phase 1/2, or +81 in phase 3), Yama's actual Magic Defence will become +10/31 respectively. The same applies to [[Zulrah]], whose tanzanite form has Magic Defence, but not its serpentine or magma forms.

==Charging==
To use the staff's magic spell, it must be charged with [[death rune|death]] and [[chaos rune|chaos]] runes ''or'' [[demon tears]], holding up to 50,000 charges when fully charged, which will last 25 hours of continuous combat. Each cast requires 2 death runes and 1 chaos rune, or 1 demon tear, costing {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Death rune|2}}+{{GEP|Chaos rune|1}}|c}} or {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Demon tear|1}}|c}} respectively per cast. Any remaining charges within the Eye can be removed at any time using the 'Uncharge' option, which will recover all runes/tears used to charge it.

The Eye cannot be charged with both runes and demon tears at the same time. If the Eye is empty or currently charged with demon tears, it will passively gain 10-20 charges upon defeating the Doom of Mokhaiotl. No game message is displayed when this occurs. If the player has multiple eyes of Ayak in their inventory upon defeating the Doom of Mokhaiotl, only one of them will be charged.

{| class="wikitable align-right-2"
!Resource 
!Cost per charge
|-
|2 x {{plink|Death rune}}
|{{Coins|{{GEP|Death rune}} * 2}}
|-
|1 x {{plink|Chaos rune}}
|{{Coins|{{GEP|Chaos rune}} * 1}}
|-
! '''Total cost per charge'''
| style="text-align:right;" | {{Coins|({{GEP|Death rune}} * 2)+({{GEP|Chaos rune}} * 1)}}
|}

The total cost to fully charge the staff with 100,000 death and 50,000 chaos runes is {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Death rune|100000}}+{{GEP|Chaos rune|50000}}|c}}. Assuming non-stop use, with one cast every 1.8 seconds equalling 2,000 casts per hour, the Eye costs {{NoCoins|({{GEP|Death rune|2}}+{{GEP|Chaos rune}})* 2000|c}} per hour to use with runes.

{| class="wikitable align-right-2"
!Resource 
!Cost per charge
|-
|1 x {{plink|Demon tear}}
|{{Coins|{{GEP|Demon tear|1}}}}
|-
! '''Total cost per charge'''
| style="text-align:right;" | {{Coins|{{GEP|Demon tear|1}}}}
|}

The total cost to fully charge the staff with 50,000 demon tears is {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Demon tear|50000}}|c}}. Assuming non-stop use, with one cast every 1.8 seconds equaling 2,000 casts per hour, the Eye costs {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Demon tear}} * 2000|c}} per hour to use with tears.

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 14 January 2026
|update = New Player Improvements Round 2
|change =
* Attacking [[Akkha]] and [[Akkha's Shadow]]s with the eye of Ayak no longer causes him to turn invisible.
* Attacking [[Yama]] with the eye of Ayak no longer causes the player to turn invisible.
}}
{{Subject changes
|update = Varlamore & Summer Sweep-Up Combat Tweaks
|date = 6 August 2025
|change = 
* The eye of Ayak now instantly kills magic grubs and ignores attack delay during the fight with the [[Doom of Mokhaiotl]].
* Using the eye of Ayak's [[special attack]] no longer causes the clickbox to change.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 30 July 2025
|type = hotfix
|change = Fixed a bug where the eye of Ayak special attack was incorrectly reducing Magic defence below 0.
}}
